name: Phase 22 — Enterprise Security Platform
summary: Full enterprise security layer implemented on top of Reno System. Includes brute force protection with account lockout, MFA login completion, fine-grained RBAC permission middleware, comprehensive security routes suite, and AI-powered anomaly detection with real risk scoring.

newModels.name: CoreTenantSecurityPolicy
newModels.description: Per-tenant configurable security policy — password rules, lockout settings, session limits, MFA requirements, IP allowlist

newModels.name: CorePasswordHistory
newModels.description: Password history per user to enforce password reuse prevention (configurable count)

newModels.name: SecLoginAttempt
newModels.description: Audit trail of every login attempt — success/failure, IP, user agent, fail reason

newModels.name: SecSecurityEvent
newModels.description: Security events with severity levels (critical/high/medium/low/info), resolve tracking

newModels.name: SecIpRule
newModels.description: IP allowlist and blocklist rules in CIDR notation, with optional expiry

bruteForceProtection.description: Login route now tracks failed attempts and locks accounts
flow: 1. Get tenant security policy (or use defaults: maxFailedAttempts=5, lockoutDurationMins=15)
2. Check IP rules — blocklist CIDR match blocks, allowlist CIDR match required if enabled
3. Check account lockout: if lockedUntil > now, return 429 with minutes remaining
4. On failed password: increment failedLoginAttempts, lock account if threshold reached
5. On lockout: create SecSecurityEvent (severity: high) for admin visibility
bruteForceProtection.loginAttemptRecording: Every login attempt (success + failure) recorded in sec_login_attempts with IP, user agent, fail reason

mfaLoginCompletion.description: POST /v1/auth/mfa/login — completes the 2-step MFA login flow
flow: 1. Accept { code: string (6 digits), tempToken: string }
2. Verify tempToken is a valid JWT with sid='temp'
3. Look up user by sub from token payload
5. Create full session, return access+refresh tokens

passwordHistoryEnforcement.description: Password change now checks history to prevent reuse
passwordHistoryEnforcement.behavior: Checks last N password hashes (N = policy.passwordHistoryCount, default 5), stores old hash in CorePasswordHistory before updating

ipRuleEngine.description: Per-tenant IP allowlist/blocklist using CIDR notation
ipRuleEngine.cidrMatching: IPv4 CIDR with automatic stripping of IPv6-mapped IPv4 addresses (::ffff: prefix)
logic: Blocklist checked first — any match returns 403
Allowlist check only when ipAllowlistEnabled=true — IP must match at least one allow rule

requirePermissionMiddleware.description: Factory middleware for fine-grained RBAC checks
requirePermissionMiddleware.signature: requirePermission(module: string, resource: string, action: string)

endpoints.description: Security overview: failed/successful logins today, active sessions, locked accounts, open events, API key count, event severity breakdown
Get tenant security policy (returns defaults if none set)
Update security policy — requires security:policy:update permission
endpoints.schema: PolicyUpdateSchema (passwordMinLength, maxFailedAttempts, lockoutDurationMins, mfaRequired, ipAllowlistEnabled, etc.)

endpoints.description: List API keys for tenant (prefix shown, full key never returned after creation)
Create API key — returns full key ONCE only — requires security:api-keys:create
List all IP rules for tenant
Create IP rule (type: allow|block, cidr) — requires security:ip-rules:create
List security events with pagination, filter by severity and resolved status
Mark security event as resolved with optional note — requires security:events:update
Paginated login attempt history with success/failure filter
List currently locked user accounts with lockUntil timestamp
Admin unlock — requires security:accounts:update. Creates info security event.
AI anomaly detection and risk intelligence

aiAnomalyDetection.description: Real-time login behavior analysis with risk scoring
aiAnomalyDetection.output: tenantRiskScore (0-100), anomalousUsers sorted by risk, recommendations array, summary stats

filesCreated: apps/api/src/rest/routes/auth.routes.ts (updated — brute force protection, MFA login, IP rules, password history)

securityDesignDecisions.decision: Default policy values baked in — no policy record required
securityDesignDecisions.rationale: New tenants have sensible defaults (5 failures, 15min lockout) without needing explicit setup
Admin users should never be accidentally locked out of security management

securityDesignDecisions.decision: API key full value shown ONCE at creation
securityDesignDecisions.rationale: Industry standard — Stripe, GitHub, AWS all work this way. Key is SHA-256 hashed at rest.

securityDesignDecisions.decision: IP rules use CIDR with IPv6-mapped-IPv4 stripping
securityDesignDecisions.rationale: Node.js/Fastify returns ::ffff:127.0.0.1 for localhost. Stripping prefix allows IPv4 CIDR rules to work correctly in Node environments

securityDesignDecisions.decision: MFA temp token reuses existing JWT with sid='temp' sentinel
securityDesignDecisions.rationale: Avoids new token type — verifyAccessToken already validates signature. The sid='temp' sentinel identifies it as incomplete auth.

securityDesignDecisions.decision: Login attempts recorded for ALL outcomes including MFA-required
securityDesignDecisions.rationale: Provides full audit trail. MFA-required entries use failReason='mfa_required' to distinguish from failures

nextPhase.recommendation: Reno Analytics Intelligence Platform — real-time cross-module analytics with predictive forecasting, custom dashboards, and AI-driven business insights
